title: 20:32 â September 3, 2020
body_text:
20:32 â September 3, 2020
Update to CPS Admin User Management Forms
We are in the process of updating our documentation to reflect the decommissioning of biometric devices. We are initially publishing changes to the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918, and the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035, but updates to other content will follow.
To request a new CPS Admin Security Officer, please use the updated version of the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918, and submit it to Central 1.
To delete or modify a CPS Admin Security Officer, you can submit the updated version of the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035, to Central 1, or you can ask another CPS Admin Security Officer to make the change on the User Management Application. CPS Admin Security Officers can delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers except for:
â¢	resetting the password, and
â¢	assigning administrative rights to applications (which gives the CPS Admin Security Officer the ability to assign those applications to others).
Refer to the secure site news item
Decommissioning of Biometric Devices for Step-Up Authentication
for more information.
Document Library
Status
CPS Admin Appointment Certificate
1918
We stated that new CPS Admin Security Officers will have access to both CPS Admin and the User Management application.
We noted that 2-step security tokens are supplied by your financial institution, not by Central 1.
We removed references to biometrics.
Revised
CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request
2035
We stated that access will be updated or deleted, as applicable, on both CPS Admin and the User Management application.
We noted that most of the change requests on the form can be completed by another CPS Admin Security Officer on the User Management application.
We updated the list of modification request types.
Revised
Questions
